message:
harden manifest generation, self-update safety, and curl/apt robustness

Manifest generator (generate.ts):
- Single-quote all bash associative array keys (['module.id'] instead of
  [module.id]) to prevent potential word-splitting or glob expansion in
  edge cases. Updates all 10 ACFS_MODULE_* associative arrays.
- Allow ${TARGET_HOME:-/home/ubuntu} and ${TARGET_USER:-ubuntu} as
  expandable variables in shellQuoteVerifiedInstallerArg(), so paths with
  default fallbacks are properly double-quoted for shell expansion rather
  than single-quoted as literal strings.
- Remove unnecessary \r \n \t escaping from escapeBash(); these control
  characters never appear in our module metadata and the escaping added
  noise without security benefit.

Self-update (scripts/lib/update.sh):
- Replace risky stash-pull-pop and reset --mixed fallback in
  update_acfs_self() with a clean skip-on-dirty-worktree policy. This
  avoids merge conflicts, unexpected rewrites of local work, and
  destructive resets during automated updates.
- Extract apt lock checking into apt_lock_is_held() and
  apt_lock_holder_details() helper functions. Both try plain fuser first
  and fall back to non-interactive sudo (sudo -n) to avoid hanging in
  safe mode or CI environments. Replaces all inline fuser calls in
  wait_for_apt_lock() and check_apt_lock().

acfs-update script resolution:
- Add relative-path lookup (SCRIPT_DIR/../scripts/lib/update.sh) before
  the $HOME/.acfs fallback, so acfs-update finds update.sh correctly
  when invoked from the installed bin directory.

GitHub API (scripts/lib/github_api.sh):
- Build curl_base_args locally in github_fetch_with_backoff() instead of
  expanding ${ACFS_CURL_BASE_ARGS[@]:-} which produces a blank argument
  when the array is unset, causing curl to fail before making a request.
  Falls back to sensible defaults (-fsSL, with --proto if supported).

Webhook (scripts/lib/webhook.sh):
- Use explicit https://ifconfig.me/ip endpoint instead of bare
  ifconfig.me to ensure HTTPS and return only the IP body.

Generated output:
- Regenerated install_stack.sh reflecting the TARGET_HOME fallback fix
  for stack.mcp_agent_mail (double-quoted expansion instead of
  single-quoted literal).
- Regenerated manifest_index.sh reflecting single-quoted associative
  array keys across all module maps.

Tests:
- generate.test.ts: Update all associative array key assertions to match
  new single-quoted format. Add regression test verifying install_stack.sh
  keeps TARGET_HOME fallback as an expandable variable.
- test_update.bats: Add two tests for apt_lock_is_held() covering plain
  fuser success and sudo -n fallback paths using stub executables.
- test_github_api.sh: Add regression test that github_fetch_with_backoff
  works when ACFS_CURL_BASE_ARGS is completely unset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -25,7 +25,16 @@ find_update_script() {
     return 0
   fi
 
-  # Installed path (after install.sh)
+  # Installed path (relative to ~/.acfs/bin/acfs-update)
+  if [[ -f "${SCRIPT_DIR}/../scripts/lib/update.sh" ]]; then
+    candidate="${SCRIPT_DIR}/../scripts/lib/update.sh"
+    # normalize path
+    candidate="$(cd "$(dirname "$candidate")" && pwd)/$(basename "$candidate")"
+    echo "$candidate"
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  # Installed path (after install.sh fallback)
   if [[ -f "$HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh" ]]; then
     candidate="$HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh"
     echo "$candidate"

@@ -233,7 +233,7 @@ install_stack_mcp_agent_mail() {
             run_as_target tmux kill-session -t "$tmux_session" 2>/dev/null || true
 
             # Create new detached tmux session and run the installer
-            if run_as_target tmux new-session -d -s "$tmux_session" 'bash' "$tmp_install" '--dir' '${TARGET_HOME:-/home/ubuntu}/mcp_agent_mail' '--yes'; then
+            if run_as_target tmux new-session -d -s "$tmux_session" 'bash' "$tmp_install" '--dir' "${TARGET_HOME:-/home/ubuntu}"'/mcp_agent_mail' '--yes'; then
                     log_success "stack.mcp_agent_mail installing in tmux session '$tmux_session'"
                     log_info "Attach with: tmux attach -t $tmux_session"
                     # Give it a moment to start
